judge: claude (cross-family; extractor is gpt-4o-mini, so judge and extractor share no model family)
deployedPipeline: full gate (A5 configuration) as served by the production app

defects.evidence: "Apologize first when there's any inconvenience..." delivered after user fragment "I think"; also "Start with calm acknowledgement, a clear apology" (user replied "you have changed the question")
"Nicely put." and "That's clear, thank you." — both times user had to type "Continue" to restart

"Okay, let me think about how to connect those details..."; "I'll quickly frame how this session will run... Got it, thank you for that"; "let's pick up right where that moment happened"; transition announces "loyalty cues" but asks about operator habits
"How do you handle delayed checkouts? When do you apply late fees, and when do you waive them..." plus six similar compound turns (msgs c5805935, f1fb1abd, 71b8b673, 60b0eda0, b353ed8b, 8e1b5ae9)
second halves never revisited: repeat-vs-first-timer needs (71b8b673), how staff prevent trust-destroying moments (60b0eda0), staffing/shift-coordination consistency (b353ed8b)
pacing reply "Yeah more that" never clarified; agent proceeded assuming depth-with-examples (which it did honor in practice)
user utterance "World" got no agent response; user had to self-recover with "Move on"

wins: Recovered the cut-off cigarette story across three consecutive turns, restating context each time until the full anecdote was elicited
Re-asked the 'small details' question with a concrete reframe (room setup, timing, greetings) when the first answer missed the intent
Pushed past a too-broad check-in-time answer to concrete deciding factors and the 12-noon default
Followed up on the unanswered late-fee/waiver half after the user only answered the checkout-pressure part
Consistent section-boundary check-ins ('Anything you'd like to add before we move on?') giving the expert control of pacing
Asked scope consent and pacing preference up front; probing style (examples, depth) was consistent with the expert's stated preference
Closing turn accurately summarized all covered areas and offered to add/correct/expand before concluding

graphCoherence.summary: Audited 57 facts and 40 edges in /workspaces/Chatgraph-V2/data/live_audit/facts-20260722-110534.json for internal semantic coherence. Found 19 incoherent facts: 5 high, 9 medium, 5 low. The high-severity cluster centers on overwritten vertices whose current names invert their relations — a LoyaltyDriver renamed 'theft' that loyal guests are 'driven by' and that positive loyalty moments 'shape'; a ServiceFailure named 'cigarette violation' whose trace and recovery edge concern unclean toilets; a CheckInPolicy whose standardTime field stores a card/cash payment rule; a DecisionRule whose late-fee-waiver text duplicates an ExceptionRule while its trace and triggering signal concern early check-in; and two CheckInPolicy vertices asserting contradictory standard times (1 p.m. vs 3:00 PM) with the latter's times unsupported by its trace. Medium findings are mislabeled or cross-wired facts (recovery advice as a TimingRule governed by check-in policies, an annoyed-guest signal marked high-value with 0.8 return likelihood, a ServiceStandard whose text denies being a standard, resolvedBy/leadsTo edges linking unrelated incidents). Low findings are field-type abuse, near-duplicate vertices, and a role tenure attached as business tenure.
